nutrient_source.title: Chicken Wings, Fried, Hot & Spicy, Fast Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient data for 'Chicken Wings, Fried, Hot & Spicy, Fast Food' (FDC ID 747517) used as a close match for 'Spicy Wow Wings'. Values adapted proportionally; core macros and secondary nutrients are representative of a breaded, deep-fried spicy chicken wing product.

gi_source.title: Chicken Nuggets
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI estimated from 'Chicken Nuggets' (GI 45, low category) as a comparable breaded, fried chicken product from the University of Sydney GI Database, due to lack of direct GI data for 'Spicy Wow Wings'. GI 45 falls within the low GI range (≤55); gi_category set to 'low' accordingly.

hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption due to high sodium content (600 mg per 100 g), which can significantly elevate blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index but high fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: While the glycemic index is low (45), the high fat content may impair insulin sensitivity; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume in moderation as part of a carbohydrate-controlled diet.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, and cholesterol
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: High saturated fat (4 g/100 g), trans fat (0.5 g/100 g), and cholesterol (100 mg/100 g) contribute to elevated LDL cholesterol and increased cardiovascular risk; intake should be strictly limited.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Spicy Wow Wings (5pcs) are a popular fast-food snack consisting of five pieces of deep-fried chicken wings coated in a spicy seasoning blend. The wings are typically marinated or tossed in a chili-based spice mixture, then deep-fried until the exterior is crispy and golden brown while the interior remains juicy and tender. The flavour profile is bold, piquant, and savory, with heat from chili and depth from garlic and paprika. Commonly served as an appetizer, snack, or side dish in Western fast-food and casual dining settings. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides approximately 280 kcal, 20 g protein, 18 g fat, and 9 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is high at 600 mg per 100 g, making this a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low category), analogous to breaded fried chicken nuggets. Saturated fat and trans fat levels are clinically relevant for cardiovascular disease risk. Suitable as an occasional treat rather than a regular dietary staple.
common_user_queries: how many calories in 5 chicken wings

confidence_reason: Nutrient data is adapted from a close analogous food in USDA FoodData Central (fast-food spicy fried chicken wings, FDC ID 747517). GI is an analogous estimate from chicken nuggets (GI 45) per the University of Sydney GI Database. Multilingual names are standard translations for 'spicy chicken wings'. No exact product match found in primary regional sources.
